/dev12000/status/flags/binary.Description: A set of binary flags giving an indication of the state of various parts of the device. Reserved for future use.

/dev12000/status/adc0min.Description: The minimum value on Signal Input 1 (ADC0) during 100 ms

/dev12000/status/adc0max.Description: The maximum value on Signal Input 1 (ADC0) during 100 ms.

/dev12000/status/adc1min.Description: The minimum value on Signal Input 2 (ADC1) during 100 ms

/dev12000/status/adc1max.Description: The maximum value on Signal Input 2 (ADC1) during 100 ms.

/dev12000/stats/physical/overtemperature.Description: This flag is set to a value greater than 0 when the internal temperatures are reaching critical limits.

/dev12000/system/update.Description: Requests update of the device firmware and bitstream from the dataserver.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/saveip.Description: If written, this action will program the defined static IP address to the device.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/defaultip4.Description: IPv4 address of the device to use if static IP is enabled.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/defaultmask.Description: IPv4 mask in case of static IP.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/defaultgateway.Description: Default gateway configuration for the network connection.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/static.Description: Enable this flag if the device is used in a network with fixed IP assignment without a DHCP server.

/dev12000/system/nics/0/mac.Description: Current MAC address of the device network interface.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/saveip.Description: If written, this action will program the defined static IP address to the device.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/defaultip4.Description: IPv4 address of the device to use if static IP is enabled.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/defaultmask.Description: IPv4 mask in case of static IP.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/defaultgateway.Description: Default gateway configuration for the network connection.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/static.Description: Enable this flag if the device is used in a network with fixed IP assignment without a DHCP server.

/dev12000/system/nics/1/mac.Description: Current MAC address of the device network interface.

/dev12000/system/stall.Description: Indicates if the network connection is stalled.

/dev12000/system/identify.Description: Setting this node to 1 will cause all frontpanel LEDs to blink for 5 seconds, then return to their previous state.

/dev12000/features/code.Description: Node providing a mechanism to write feature codes.

/dev12000/system/properties/timebase.Description: Minimal time difference between two timestamps. The value is equal to 1/(maximum sampling rate).

/dev12000/system/properties/freqresoluti.Description: The number of bits used to represent a frequency.

/dev12000/system/properties/negativefreq.Description: Indicates whether negative frequencies are supported.

/dev12000/system/properties/minfreq.Description: The minimum oscillator frequency that can be set.

/dev12000/system/properties/maxfreq.Description: The maximum oscillator frequency that can be set.

/dev12000/system/properties/mintimeconst.Description: The minimum demodulator time constant that can be set. Only relevant for lock-in amplifiers.

/dev12000/system/properties/maxtimeconst.Description: The maximum demodulator time constant that can be set. Only relevant for lock-in amplifiers.

/dev12000/system/properties/freqscaling.Description: The scale factor to use to convert a frequency represented as a freqresolution-bit integer to a floating point value.

/dev12000/system/properties/maxdemodrate.Description: The maximum demodulator rate that can be set. Only relevant for lock-in amplifiers.

/dev12000/system/swtriggers/0/single.Description: Issues a single software trigger event.

/dev12000/system/fwlogenable.Description: Enables logging to the fwlog node.

/dev12000/system/boardrevisions/0.Description: Hardware revision of the motherboard containing the FPGA.

/dev12000/system/boardrevisions/1.Description: Hardware revision of the Signal Output board.

/dev12000/system/shutdown.Description: Sending a '1' to this node initiates a shutdown of the operating system on the device. It is recommended to trigger this shutdown before switching the device off with the hardware switch at the back side of the device.

/dev12000/system/clocks/referenceclock/i.Description: The intended reference clock source. When the source is changed, all the instruments connected with ZSync links will be disconnected. The connection should be re-established manually.

Options.0: "internal": The internal clock is intended to be used as the frequency and time base reference.
Options.1: "external": An external clock is intended to be used as the frequency and time base reference. Provide a clean and stable 10 MHz or 100 MHz reference to the appropriate back panel connector.
Options.2: "zsync": The ZSync clock is intended to be used as the frequency and time base reference.

Options.0: "internal": The internal clock is used as the frequency and time base reference.
Options.1: "external": An external clock is used as the frequency and time base reference.
Options.2: "zsync": The ZSync clock is used as the frequency and time base reference.

Options.0: "locked": Reference clock has been locked on.
Options.1: "error": There was an error locking onto the reference clock signal.
Options.2: "busy": The device is busy trying to lock onto the reference clock signal.

/dev12000/system/clocks/referenceclock/i.Description: Indicates the frequency of the reference clock.

/dev12000/system/clocks/referenceclock/o.Description: Enable clock signal on the reference clock output. When the clock output is turned on or off, all the instruments connected with ZSync links will be disconnected. The connection should be re-established manually.
Select the frequency of the output reference clock. Only 10 MHz and 100 MHz are allowed.

/dev12000/system/fwlog.Description: Returns log output of the firmware.

/dev12000/system/preset/busy.Description: Indicates if presets are currently loaded.

/dev12000/system/preset/error.Description: Indicates if the last operation was illegal. Successful: 0, Error: 1.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/centerfreq.Description: The Center Frequency of the analysis band.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/input/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Input power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Output power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/filter.Description: Reads the selected analog filter before the Signal Output.

Options.1: "lowpass_3000": Low-pass filter of 3 GHz.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/triggers/0/imp50.Description: Trigger Input impedance: When on, the Trigger Input impedance is 50 Ohm; when off, 1 kOhm.

Options.1: "50_Ohm": ON: 50 Ohm

/dev12000/qachannels/0/triggers/0/value.Description: Shows the value of the digital Trigger Input. The value is integrated over a period of 100 ms. Values are: 1: low; 2: high; 3: was low and high in the period.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/markers/0/source.Description: Selects the source for the marker output.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/triggers/1/imp50.Description: Trigger Input impedance: When on, the Trigger Input impedance is 50 Ohm; when off, 1 kOhm.

Options.1: "50_Ohm": ON: 50 Ohm

/dev12000/qachannels/0/triggers/1/value.Description: Shows the value of the digital Trigger Input. The value is integrated over a period of 100 ms. Values are: 1: low; 2: high; 3: was low and high in the period.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/markers/1/source.Description: Selects the source for the marker output.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/mode.Description: Selects between Spectroscopy and Qubit Readout mode.

Options.0: "spectroscopy": In Spectroscopy mode, the Signal Output is connected to the Oscillator, with which also the measured signals are correlated.
Options.1: "readout": In Qubit Readout mode, the Signal Output is connected to the Readout Pulse Generator, and the measured signals are correlated with the Integration Weights before state discrimination.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/input/overrangeco.Description: Indicates the number of times the Signal Input was in an overrange condition within intervals of 200 ms. The overrange condition can cause potential damage of the Signal Input processing electronics of the instrument.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/overrangec.Description: Indicates the number of times the Signal Output was in an overrange condition within the last 200 ms. It is checked for an overrange condition every 10 ms.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/input/adcoverrang.Description: Indicates the number of times the analog-to-digital converter (ADC) of the Signal Input was in an overrange condition within intervals of 200 ms. Note that this condition always occurs together with the condition of the "overrangecount" node. The overrange condition can cause potential damage of the Signal Input processing electronics, in particular the ADC units.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/input/rflfpath.Description: Switch between RF and LF path for the QA channel input.

Options.1: "rf": RF path is used.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/rflfpath.Description: Switch between RF and LF path for the QA channel output.

Options.1: "rf": RF path is used.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/input/digitalmixe.Description: The Center Frequency of the digital mixer for the Signal Input.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/digitalmix.Description: The Center Frequency of the digital mixer for the Signal Output.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/output/rflfinterl.Description: Enables the RF/LF path interlock between input and output. If enabled (1), the output path is always configured according to the input. The default value is disabled (0).

/dev12000/qachannels/1/centerfreq.Description: The Center Frequency of the analysis band.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/input/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Input power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Output power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/filter.Description: Reads the selected analog filter before the Signal Output.

Options.1: "lowpass_3000": Low-pass filter of 3 GHz.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/triggers/0/imp50.Description: Trigger Input impedance: When on, the Trigger Input impedance is 50 Ohm; when off, 1 kOhm.

Options.1: "50_Ohm": ON: 50 Ohm

/dev12000/qachannels/1/triggers/0/value.Description: Shows the value of the digital Trigger Input. The value is integrated over a period of 100 ms. Values are: 1: low; 2: high; 3: was low and high in the period.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/markers/0/source.Description: Selects the source for the marker output.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/triggers/1/imp50.Description: Trigger Input impedance: When on, the Trigger Input impedance is 50 Ohm; when off, 1 kOhm.

Options.1: "50_Ohm": ON: 50 Ohm

/dev12000/qachannels/1/triggers/1/value.Description: Shows the value of the digital Trigger Input. The value is integrated over a period of 100 ms. Values are: 1: low; 2: high; 3: was low and high in the period.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/markers/1/source.Description: Selects the source for the marker output.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/mode.Description: Selects between Spectroscopy and Qubit Readout mode.

Options.0: "spectroscopy": In Spectroscopy mode, the Signal Output is connected to the Oscillator, with which also the measured signals are correlated.
Options.1: "readout": In Qubit Readout mode, the Signal Output is connected to the Readout Pulse Generator, and the measured signals are correlated with the Integration Weights before state discrimination.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/input/overrangeco.Description: Indicates the number of times the Signal Input was in an overrange condition within intervals of 200 ms. The overrange condition can cause potential damage of the Signal Input processing electronics of the instrument.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/overrangec.Description: Indicates the number of times the Signal Output was in an overrange condition within the last 200 ms. It is checked for an overrange condition every 10 ms.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/input/adcoverrang.Description: Indicates the number of times the analog-to-digital converter (ADC) of the Signal Input was in an overrange condition within intervals of 200 ms. Note that this condition always occurs together with the condition of the "overrangecount" node. The overrange condition can cause potential damage of the Signal Input processing electronics, in particular the ADC units.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/input/rflfpath.Description: Switch between RF and LF path for the QA channel input.

Options.1: "rf": RF path is used.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/rflfpath.Description: Switch between RF and LF path for the QA channel output.

Options.1: "rf": RF path is used.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/input/digitalmixe.Description: The Center Frequency of the digital mixer for the Signal Input.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/digitalmix.Description: The Center Frequency of the digital mixer for the Signal Output.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/output/rflfinterl.Description: Enables the RF/LF path interlock between input and output. If enabled (1), the output path is always configured according to the input. The default value is disabled (0).

/dev12000/qachannels/2/centerfreq.Description: The Center Frequency of the analysis band.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/input/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Input power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/output/range.Description: Sets the maximal Range of the Signal Output power. The instrument selects the closest available Range with a resolution of 5 dBm.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/output/filter.Description: Reads the selected analog filter before the Signal Output.

Options.1: "lowpass_3000": Low-pass filter of 3 GHz.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/le.Description: Number of data points to record. One data point corresponds to a single averaged result value of the selected source.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/av.Description: Number of measurements that are averaged. Only powers of 2 are valid, other values are rounded down to the next power of 2. 1 means no averaging. The maximum setting is 32768.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/ac.Description: Indicates the index of the acquisition that will be performed on the next trigger.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/en.Description: Enables the acquisition of readout results.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/so.Description: Selects the signal source of the Result Logger.

Options.1: "result_of_integration": Complex-valued integration results of the Weighted Integration in Qubit Readout mode.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/er.Description: Number of hold-off errors detected since last reset.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/spectroscopy/leng.Description: Sets the integration length in Spectroscopy mode in number of samples. Up to 33.5 MSa (2^25 samples) can be recorded, which corresponds to 16.7 ms.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/integrati.Description: Sets the length of all Integration Weights in number of samples. A maximum of 4096 samples can be integrated, which corresponds to 2.05 us.
Sets a common delay for the start of the readout integration for all Integration Weights with respect to the time when the trigger is received. The resolution is 2 ns.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/spectroscopy/dela.Description: Sets the delay of the integration in Spectroscopy mode with respect to the Trigger signal. The resolution is 2 ns.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/spectroscopy/trig.Description: Selects the source of the trigger for the integration and envelope in Spectroscopy mode.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/spectroscopy/resu.Description: Number of data points to record. One data point corresponds to a single averaged result value of the selected source.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/ov.Description: Number of DIO interface overflows during readout. This can happen if readouts are triggered faster than the maximum possible data-rate of the DIO interface.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/readout/result/mo.Description: Selects the averaging order of the result.

Options.0: "cyclic": Cyclic averaging: a sequence of multiple results is recorded first, then averaged with the next repetition of the same sequence.
Options.1: "sequential": Sequential averaging: each result is recorded and averaged first, before the next result is recorded and averaged.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/single.Description: Puts the Sequencer into single-shot mode.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/ready.Description: The Sequencer has a compiled program and is ready to be enabled.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/sequenc.Description: Status of the Sequencer on the instrument. Bit 0: Sequencer is running; Bit 1: reserved; Bit 2: Sequencer is waiting for a trigger to arrive; Bit 3: Sequencer has detected an error; Bit 4: sequencer is waiting for synchronization with other channels.
Size of the current Sequencer program relative to the available instruction memory of 16 kInstructions (16'384 instructions).
When at value 1, indicates that the Sequencer has been triggered.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/reset.Description: Clears the configured Sequencer program and resets the state to not ready.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/elf/len.Description: Length of the compiled ELF file.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/elf/pro.Description: Percentage of the Sequencer program already uploaded to the device.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/dio/val.Description: Select the DIO bit to use as the VALID signal to indicate that a valid input is available.
Polarity of the VALID bit that indicates that a valid input is available.

Options.1: "low": Low: VALID bit must be logical zero.
Options.2: "high": High: VALID bit must be logical high.
Options.3: "both": Both: VALID bit may be logical high or zero.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/elf/dat.Description: Accepts the data of the sequencer ELF file. If the sequence pipeliner mode is not off, the data of the ELF file goes to the staging area of the sequence pipeliner instead.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/sequenc.Description: Displays the source code of the current Sequencer program.
Displays the current sequence program in compiled form. Every line corresponds to one hardware instruction.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/elf/nam.Description: Name of the uploaded ELF file.

Options.0: "normal": Normal: Logger starts with the AWG and overwrites old values as soon as the memory limit of 1024 entries is reached.
Options.1: "timestamp": Timestamp-triggered: Logger starts with the AWG, waits for the first valid trigger, and only starts recording data after the time specified by the starttimestamp. Recording stops as soon as the memory limit of 1024 entries is reached.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/rtlogge.Description: Timestamp at which to start logging for timestamp-triggered mode.

Options.1: "normal": Normal: Logger is running in normal mode.
Options.2: "ts_wait": Wait for timestamp: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and waits for start timestamp.
Options.3: "ts_active": Active: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and logging.
Options.4: "ts_full": Log Full: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and has stopped logging because log is full.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/rtlogge.Description: Minimal time difference between two timestamps. The value matches the AWG sequencer execution rate (4 ns).

Options.0: "dio": DIO interface will be used as input.
Options.1: "zsync": ZSync interface will be used as input.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/rtlogge.Description: Vector node with the logged events.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/mode.Description: Selects the sequence pipeliner mode: off (default), batch, or queue mode. Changing the mode will reset both the sequence pipeliner and the normal AWG.

Options.0: "off": Off: The sequence pipeliner is turned off.
Options.1: "batch": Batch: The sequence pipeliner operates in batch mode. All sequences must be committed before the pipeliner is enabled. A batch can be executed once or multiple times.
Options.2: "queue": Queue: The sequence pipeliner operates in queue mode. Sequences can be committed while the pipeliner is enabled. Every sequence is executed only once and the slot in the queue is then available for a new sequence.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/repetit.Description: Number of batch repetitions (1 to 4e6). This node is fixed to 1 if the sequence pipeliner is not in batch mode.
Number of remaining batch repetitions. This node is fixed to 1 if the sequence pipeliner is not in batch mode.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/commit.Description: Commit node data in staging area to queue of sequence pipeliner.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/enable.Description: Enable execution of sequences in pipeline.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/reset.Description: Clears all sequences previously added to the sequence pipeliner and disables the pipeliner if it has been running before.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/idcurre.Description: ID of sequence in staging area.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/status.Description: Status of the sequence pipeliner (0: idle, 1: executing sequence, 2: waiting for next sequence to be committed (queue mode only), 3: done (batch mode only)

Options.0: "idle": Idle: The sequence pipeliner is idle.
Options.1: "exec": Executing sequence: The sequence pipeliner is executing a sequence.
Options.2: "waiting": Waiting: The sequence pipeliner is waiting for the next sequence to be committed (queue mode only).
Options.3: "done": Done: The sequence pipeliner is still enabled but all sequences have been executed (batch mode only).

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/availab.Description: Number of free slots in the sequence pipeliner queue. Sequence upload is blocked if this node is 0.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/maxslot.Description: Maximum number of available slots in the sequence pipeliner queue.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/timeout.Description: Maximal execution time per sequence in milliseconds. The execution of a sequence is aborted if the maximal execution time is reached. A value of 0 means infinity.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/synchronization/e.Description: Enable multi-channel synchronization for this channel. The program will only execute once all channels with enabled synchronization are ready.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/pipeliner/ready.Description: Indicates whether a sequence can be committed to the pipeliner.

Bit 0: Sequencer is running; Bit 1: reserved; Bit 2: Sequencer is waiting for a trigger to arrive; Bit 3: Sequencer has detected an error; Bit 4: sequencer is waiting for synchronization with other channels.
Size of the current Sequencer program relative to the available instruction memory of 16 kInstructions (16'384 instructions).
When at value 1, indicates that the Sequencer has been triggered.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/reset.Description: Clears the configured Sequencer program and resets the state to not ready.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/elf/len.Description: Length of the compiled ELF file.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/elf/pro.Description: Percentage of the Sequencer program already uploaded to the device.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/dio/val.Description: Select the DIO bit to use as the VALID signal to indicate that a valid input is available.
Polarity of the VALID bit that indicates that a valid input is available.

Options.1: "low": Low: VALID bit must be logical zero.
Options.2: "high": High: VALID bit must be logical high.
Options.3: "both": Both: VALID bit may be logical high or zero.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/elf/dat.Description: Accepts the data of the sequencer ELF file. If the sequence pipeliner mode is not off, the data of the ELF file goes to the staging area of the sequence pipeliner instead.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/sequenc.Description: Displays the source code of the current Sequencer program.
Displays the current sequence program in compiled form. Every line corresponds to one hardware instruction.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/elf/nam.Description: Name of the uploaded ELF file.

Options.0: "normal": Normal: Logger starts with the AWG and overwrites old values as soon as the memory limit of 1024 entries is reached.
Options.1: "timestamp": Timestamp-triggered: Logger starts with the AWG, waits for the first valid trigger, and only starts recording data after the time specified by the starttimestamp. Recording stops as soon as the memory limit of 1024 entries is reached.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/rtlogge.Description: Timestamp at which to start logging for timestamp-triggered mode.

Options.1: "normal": Normal: Logger is running in normal mode.
Options.2: "ts_wait": Wait for timestamp: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and waits for start timestamp.
Options.3: "ts_active": Active: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and logging.
Options.4: "ts_full": Log Full: Logger is in timestamp-triggered mode and has stopped logging because log is full.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/rtlogge.Description: Minimal time difference between two timestamps. The value matches the AWG sequencer execution rate (4 ns).

Options.0: "dio": DIO interface will be used as input.
Options.1: "zsync": ZSync interface will be used as input.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/rtlogge.Description: Vector node with the logged events.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/mode.Description: Selects the sequence pipeliner mode: off (default), batch, or queue mode. Changing the mode will reset both the sequence pipeliner and the normal AWG.

Options.0: "off": Off: The sequence pipeliner is turned off.
Options.1: "batch": Batch: The sequence pipeliner operates in batch mode. All sequences must be committed before the pipeliner is enabled. A batch can be executed once or multiple times.
Options.2: "queue": Queue: The sequence pipeliner operates in queue mode. Sequences can be committed while the pipeliner is enabled. Every sequence is executed only once and the slot in the queue is then available for a new sequence.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/repetit.Description: Number of batch repetitions (1 to 4e6). This node is fixed to 1 if the sequence pipeliner is not in batch mode.
Number of remaining batch repetitions. This node is fixed to 1 if the sequence pipeliner is not in batch mode.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/commit.Description: Commit node data in staging area to queue of sequence pipeliner.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/enable.Description: Enable execution of sequences in pipeline.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/reset.Description: Clears all sequences previously added to the sequence pipeliner and disables the pipeliner if it has been running before.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/idcurre.Description: ID of sequence in staging area.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/status.Description: Status of the sequence pipeliner (0: idle, 1: executing sequence, 2: waiting for next sequence to be committed (queue mode only), 3: done (batch mode only)

Options.0: "idle": Idle: The sequence pipeliner is idle.
Options.1: "exec": Executing sequence: The sequence pipeliner is executing a sequence.
Options.2: "waiting": Waiting: The sequence pipeliner is waiting for the next sequence to be committed (queue mode only).
Options.3: "done": Done: The sequence pipeliner is still enabled but all sequences have been executed (batch mode only).

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/availab.Description: Number of free slots in the sequence pipeliner queue. Sequence upload is blocked if this node is 0.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/maxslot.Description: Maximum number of available slots in the sequence pipeliner queue.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/timeout.Description: Maximal execution time per sequence in milliseconds. The execution of a sequence is aborted if the maximal execution time is reached. A value of 0 means infinity.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/synchronization/e.Description: Enable multi-channel synchronization for this channel. The program will only execute once all channels with enabled synchronization are ready.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/pipeliner/ready.Description: Indicates whether a sequence can be committed to the pipeliner.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/generator/auxtrig.Description: Selects the source of the digital Trigger.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/oscs/0/freq.Description: Controls the frequency of each digital Oscillator.

/dev12000/qachannels/0/oscs/0/gain.Description: Controls the gain of each digital Oscillator. The gain is defined relative to the Output Range of the Readout Channel.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/oscs/0/freq.Description: Controls the frequency of each digital Oscillator.

/dev12000/qachannels/1/oscs/0/gain.Description: Controls the gain of each digital Oscillator. The gain is defined relative to the Output Range of the Readout Channel.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/oscs/0/freq.Description: Controls the frequency of each digital Oscillator.

/dev12000/qachannels/2/oscs/0/gain.Description: Controls the gain of each digital Oscillator. The gain is defined relative to the Output Range of the Readout Channel.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/oscs/0/freq.Description: Controls the frequency of each digital Oscillator.

/dev12000/qachannels/3/oscs/0/gain.Description: Controls the gain of each digital Oscillator. The gain is defined relative to the Output Range of the Readout Channel.

/dev12000/scopes/0/enable.Description: Enables the acquisition of Scope shots. Goes back to 0 (disabled) after the scope shot has been acquired.

/dev12000/scopes/0/length.Description: Defines the length of the recorded Scope shot in number of samples.

/dev12000/scopes/0/time.Description: Defines the time base of the Scope.

/dev12000/scopes/0/single.Description: Puts the Scope into single shot mode.

Options.1: "chan0trigin1", "channel0_trigger_input1": Channel 1, Trigger Input B.

/dev12000/scopes/0/trigger/delay.Description: The delay of a Scope measurement. A negative delay results in data being acquired before the trigger point. The resolution is 2 ns.

/dev12000/scopes/0/trigger/enable.Description: When triggering is enabled scope data are acquired every time the defined trigger condition is met.

Options.1: "on": ON: Trigger based scope shot acquisition

/dev12000/scopes/0/averaging/count.Description: Configures the number of Scope measurements to average.

/dev12000/scopes/0/segments/count.Description: Specifies the number of segments to be recorded in device memory. The maximum Scope shot size is given by the available memory divided by the number of segments.

/dev12000/scopes/0/segments/enable.Description: Enable segmented Scope recording. This allows for full bandwidth recording of Scope shots with a minimum dead time between individual shots.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/0/enable.Description: Enables recording for this Scope channel.

Options.1: "chan1sigin", "channel1_signal_input": Signal Input Channel 2.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/1/enable.Description: Enables recording for this Scope channel.

Options.1: "chan1sigin", "channel1_signal_input": Signal Input Channel 2.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/2/enable.Description: Enables recording for this Scope channel.

Options.1: "chan1sigin", "channel1_signal_input": Signal Input Channel 2.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/3/enable.Description: Enables recording for this Scope channel.

Options.1: "chan1sigin", "channel1_signal_input": Signal Input Channel 2.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/0/wave.Description: Contains the acquired Scope measurement data.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/1/wave.Description: Contains the acquired Scope measurement data.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/2/wave.Description: Contains the acquired Scope measurement data.

/dev12000/scopes/0/channels/3/wave.Description: Contains the acquired Scope measurement data.

Options.1: "on": Generator on

/dev12000/system/internaltrigger/repetit.Description: Number of triggers to be generated.

/dev12000/system/internaltrigger/progres.Description: The fraction of the triggers generated so far.

/dev12000/system/internaltrigger/synchro.Description: Enable synchronization. Trigger generation will only start once all synchronization participants have reported a ready status. Synchronization checks will be repeated with the same trigger generation settings (holdoff and repetitions) until synchronization is disabled.

/dev12000/system/synchronization/source.Description: Selects the source for synchronization of channels: internal (default) or external

Options.0: "internal": Internal: Synchronization of all channels of a device that have the corresponding synchronization setting enabled.
Options.1: "external": External: Same as internal plus synchronization to other devices via ZSync.

/dev12000/dios/0/drive.Description: When on (1), the corresponding 8-bit bus is in output mode. When off (0), it is in input mode. Bit 0 corresponds to the least significant byte. For example, the value 1 drives the least significant byte, the value 8 drives the most significant byte.

/dev12000/dios/0/output.Description: Sets the value of the DIO output for those bytes where 'drive' is enabled.

Options.0: "manual": Enables manual control of the DIO output bits.
Options.16: "qa_result": Sends discriminated readout results to the DIO.
Options.32: "qachan0seq", "qachannel0_sequencer": Enables control of DIO values by the sequencer of QA channel 1.
Options.33: "qachan1seq", "qachannel1_sequencer": Enables control of DIO values by the sequencer of QA channel 2.
Options.34: "qachan2seq", "qachannel2_sequencer": Enables control of DIO values by the sequencer of QA channel 3.
Options.35: "qachan3seq", "qachannel3_sequencer": Enables control of DIO values by the sequencer of QA channel 4.

/dev12000/dios/0/input.Description: Gives the value of the DIO input for those bytes where drive is disabled.

/dev12000/dios/0/interface.Description: Selects the interface standard to use on the 32-bit DIO interface. A value of 0 means that a 3.3 V CMOS interface is used. A value of 1 means that an LVDS compatible interface is used.

/dev12000/system/fwrevision.Description: Revision of the device-internal controller software.

/dev12000/status/fifolevel.Description: USB FIFO level: Indicates the USB FIFO fill level inside the device. When 100%, data is lost

/dev12000/clockbase.Description: Returns the internal clock frequency of the device.

/dev12000/system/owner.Description: Returns the current owner of the device (IP).

/dev12000/system/kerneltype.Description: Returns the type of the data server kernel (mdk or hpk).

/dev12000/system/activeinterface.Description: Currently active interface of the device.

/dev12000/system/interfacespeed.Description: Speed of the currently active interface (USB only).

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/bytesreceived.Description: Number of bytes received on the command stream from the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/bytessent.Description: Number of bytes sent on the command stream from the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/packetsreceive.Description: Number of packets received on the command stream from the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/packetssent.Description: Number of packets sent on the command stream to the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/packetslost.Description: Number of command packets lost since device start. Command packets contain device settings that are sent to and received from the device.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/bandwidth.Description: Command streaming bandwidth usage on the physical network connection between device and data server.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/processing.Description: Number of buffers being processed for command packets. Small values indicate proper performance. For a TCP/IP interface, command packets are sent using the TCP protocol.

/dev12000/stats/cmdstream/pending.Description: Number of buffers ready for receiving command packets from the device.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/bytesreceived.Description: Number of bytes received on the data stream from the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/packetsreceiv.Description: Number of packets received on the data stream from the device since session start.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/packetslost.Description: Number of data packets lost since device start. Data packets contain measurement data.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/bandwidth.Description: Data streaming bandwidth usage on the physical network connection between device and data server.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/processing.Description: Number of buffers being processed for data packets. Small values indicate proper performance. For a TCP/IP interface, data packets are sent using the UDP protocol.

/dev12000/stats/datastream/pending.Description: Number of buffers ready for receiving data packets from the device.

/dev12000/status/flags/packetlosstcp.Description: Flag indicating if tcp packages have been lost.

/dev12000/status/flags/packetlossudp.Description: Flag indicating if udp packages have been lost.

/zi/config/open.Description: Enable communication with the LabOne Data Server from other computers in the network.

Options.0: "local": Communication only possible with the local machine.
Options.1: "network": Communication possible with other machines in the network.

/zi/config/port.Description: The IP port on which the LabOne Data Server listens.

/zi/about/revision.Description: Contains the revision number of the Zurich Instruments Data Server.

/zi/about/version.Description: Contains the version of the LabOne software.

/zi/about/commit.Description: Contains the commit hash of the source code used to build this version of the LabOne software.

/zi/about/dataserver.Description: Contains information about the Zurich Instruments Data Server.

/zi/about/fwrevision.Description: Contains the revision of the device firmware.

/zi/debug/logpath.Description: Returns the path of the log directory.

/zi/debug/level.Description: Set the logging level (amount of detail) of the LabOne Data Server.

Options.0: "trace": Trace. Messages designated as traces are logged.
Options.1: "debug": Debug. Messages designated as debugging info are logged.
Options.2: "info": Info. Messages designated as informational are logged.
Options.3: "status": Status. Messages designated as status info are logged.
Options.4: "warning": Warning. Messages designated as warnings are logged.
Options.5: "error": Error. Messages designated as errors are logged.
Options.6: "fatal": Fatal. Messages designated as fatal errors are logged.

/zi/debug/log.Description: Returns the logfile text of the LabOne Data Server.

/zi/clockbase.Description: A fallback clock frequency that can be used by clients for calculating time bases when no other is available.

/zi/devices/visible.Description: Contains a list of devices in the network visible to the LabOne Data Server.

/zi/devices/connected.Description: Contains a list of devices connected to the LabOne Data Server.

/zi/mds/groups/0/devices.Description: Contains a list of devices in this synchronization group.

/zi/mds/groups/0/status.Description: Indicates the status the synchronization group.

Options.-1: Error. An error occurred in the synchronization process.
Options.1: Sync

/zi/mds/groups/0/locked.Description: Indicates whether the device group is locked by a MDS module.

/zi/mds/groups/0/keepalive.Description: Set by the MDS module to indicate control over this synchronization group.
